title: Supermarket Saturday
summary: A father and his daughter Lily do their weekly grocery shopping together. They work through their list, find items on the shelves, and pay at the checkout.

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Right, Lily, we need to get everything on the list. Can you hold it for me?

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Sure! What's first on the list, Dad?

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Bread and milk. They're usually at the back of the store. Let's start there.

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: I can see the milk! It's on the left, next to the butter.

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Good eye! Grab two litres of full-fat milk, please. Now, where's the bread?

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: It's on the next aisle. Look — wholemeal or white?

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Wholemeal. It's healthier. What else do we need?

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Eggs, apples, cheese, and pasta. Oh, and washing-up liquid!

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: The eggs and cheese are in the fridge section. Can you get six eggs? Not the ones at the front — they look a bit old.

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Got it. I'll take ones from the back. Dad, how many apples do we need?

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: A bag of six should be enough. And pick firm ones — not the soft ones.

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Okay! I found the pasta too. Which one — spaghetti or penne?

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Penne, please. Now let's find the washing-up liquid and we're done.

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Found it! Aisle four, near the cleaning things. Dad, can I get a magazine?

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: We'll see. Let's pay first. Self-checkout or the till?

body.speaker: Lily
body.text: Self-checkout is faster! I'll scan the things.

body.speaker: Dad
body.text: Great. Go ahead. And yes — you can get the magazine. You were a big help today.

annotations.meaningVi: lối đi giữa các kệ hàng (trong siêu thị)
annotations.exampleEn: The cereals are in aisle three, next to the biscuits.
Wholemeal bread has more fibre than white bread.

annotations.meaningVi: khu vực hàng lạnh (trong siêu thị)
annotations.exampleEn: The yoghurt is in the fridge section near the back.
Choose firm apples — soft ones are usually overripe.
I need to buy washing-up liquid — we've run out.
She used the self-checkout to avoid the long queue.
There was a short queue at the till.
Scan each item and place it in the bag.
Young children are often given full-fat milk.

annotations.meaningVi: giúp đỡ rất nhiều, trợ thủ đắc lực
annotations.exampleEn: You were a big help today — thank you so much.
Grab a trolley before we go in.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives for Shopping Instructions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mua sắm, chúng ta thường dùng câu mệnh lệnh để hướng dẫn nhau: 'Grab two litres of milk.' / 'Get six eggs.' / 'Pick firm ones.' / 'Scan the things.' Câu mệnh lệnh bắt đầu bằng động từ nguyên mẫu và không có chủ ngữ. Chúng nghe tự nhiên và không thô lỗ trong tình huống gia đình hoặc bạn bè.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Shopping conversations use many imperative (command) sentences to give instructions: 'Grab two litres of milk.' / 'Get six eggs.' / 'Pick firm ones.' Imperatives start with the base verb and have no subject. In informal family situations, imperatives sound natural and helpful, not rude.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives: -er and 'more'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ so sánh hơn được dùng để so sánh hai thứ. Tính từ ngắn thêm '-er': firm → firmer. Tính từ dài dùng 'more': healthy → healthier (trường hợp đặc biệt với '-ier'). Ví dụ trong bài: 'It's healthier.' / 'Self-checkout is faster!' Lưu ý: 'healthier' và 'faster' đều là dạng so sánh hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Comparative adjectives compare two things. Short adjectives add '-er': fast → faster. Two-syllable adjectives ending in '-y' change to '-ier': healthy → healthier. Examples in the dialogue: 'Self-checkout is faster!' / 'It's healthier.' These compare two choices to explain which is preferred.

translationVi:
Bố: Nào, Lily, chúng mình cần mua hết những thứ trong danh sách. Con có thể cầm giúp bố không?
Lily: Được ạ! Đầu tiên là gì vậy bố?
Bố: Bánh mì và sữa. Chúng thường ở phía sau siêu thị. Mình bắt đầu từ đó nhé.
Lily: Con thấy sữa rồi! Ở bên trái, cạnh bơ kìa.
Bố: Con tinh mắt thật! Lấy hai lít sữa nguyên kem nhé. Bánh mì ở đâu nhỉ?
Lily: Ở lối đi tiếp theo ạ. Kìa — bánh mì nguyên cám hay bánh mì trắng?
Bố: Bánh mì nguyên cám. Tốt cho sức khoẻ hơn. Còn cần gì nữa không?
Lily: Trứng, táo, phô mai, và mì ống ạ. À, và cả nước rửa chén nữa!
Bố: Trứng và phô mai ở khu hàng lạnh. Con lấy sáu quả trứng được không? Đừng lấy mấy quả ở phía trước — trông có vẻ hơi cũ rồi.
Lily: Vâng ạ. Con sẽ lấy mấy quả ở phía sau. Bố ơi, cần bao nhiêu quả táo?
Bố: Một túi sáu quả là đủ rồi. Chọn quả nào chắc nhé — đừng lấy quả mềm.
Lily: Vâng! Con tìm thấy mì ống rồi. Loại nào bố — spaghetti hay penne?
Bố: Penne nhé. Bây giờ tìm nước rửa chén là xong.
Lily: Tìm thấy rồi! Lối đi số bốn, gần khu đồ vệ sinh. Bố ơi, con có thể mua một tờ tạp chí không?
Bố: Xem đã. Thanh toán xong rồi tính. Tự thanh toán hay quầy thu ngân?
Lily: Quầy tự thanh toán nhanh hơn ạ! Con sẽ quét hàng cho.
Bố: Tốt lắm. Con làm đi. Và vâng — con có thể lấy tờ tạp chí. Hôm nay con giúp bố rất nhiều đó.

questions.prompt: Who is holding the shopping list?
questions.explanation: Dad asks 'Can you hold it for me?' and Lily agrees.
questions.hint: Who does Dad ask to hold the list?

questions.prompt: Where is the milk in the store?
options: At the front of the store
On the left, next to the butter
questions.explanation: Lily says 'It's on the left, next to the butter.'
questions.hint: Where does Lily spot the milk?

questions.prompt: How much milk does Dad ask Lily to get?
questions.explanation: Dad says 'Grab two litres of full-fat milk, please.'
questions.hint: How many litres does Dad specify?

questions.prompt: Which type of bread does Dad choose?
questions.hint: Which option does Dad say is healthier?

questions.prompt: Why does Dad tell Lily NOT to take the eggs at the front?
questions.explanation: Dad says 'Not the ones at the front — they look a bit old.'
questions.hint: What does Dad say is wrong with the front eggs?

questions.prompt: How many apples does Dad want?
questions.explanation: Dad says 'A bag of six should be enough.'
questions.hint: What number bag of apples does Dad suggest?

questions.prompt: What kind of apples should Lily choose?
questions.explanation: Dad says 'pick firm ones — not the soft ones.'
questions.hint: What quality should the apples have?
Which pasta shape does Dad select?

options: Aisle four, near the cleaning things
questions.explanation: Lily says 'Aisle four, near the cleaning things.'
questions.hint: What aisle and section does Lily find the washing-up liquid in?
What reason does Lily give for choosing self-checkout?

questions.prompt: Why does Dad let Lily get the magazine?
options: Because she was a big help
Because she found everything on the list
questions.explanation: Dad says 'You were a big help today.'
questions.hint: What reason does Dad give for saying yes to the magazine?

cloze.template:
Dad: Right, Lily, we need to get everything on the {{b1}}. Can you hold it for me?
Lily: Sure! What's first on the list, Dad?
Dad: Bread and milk. They're usually at the back of the store. Let's start there.
Lily: I can see the milk! It's on the left, next to the {{b2}}.
Dad: Good eye! Grab two litres of full-fat milk, please. Now, where's the bread?
Lily: It's on the next {{b3}}. Look — wholemeal or white?
Dad: Wholemeal. It's {{b4}}. What else do we need?
Lily: Eggs, apples, cheese, and pasta. Oh, and washing-up liquid!
Dad: The eggs and cheese are in the {{b5}} section. Can you get six eggs? Not the ones at the front — they look a bit old.
Lily: Got it. I'll take ones from the back. Dad, how many apples do we need?
Dad: A bag of six should be enough. And pick {{b6}} ones — not the soft ones.
Lily: Okay! I found the pasta too. Which one — spaghetti or {{b7}}?
Dad: Penne, please. Now let's find the washing-up liquid and we're done.
Lily: Found it! Aisle four, near the cleaning things. Dad, can I get a {{b8}}?
Dad: We'll see. Let's pay first. Self-checkout or the {{b9}}?
Lily: Self-checkout is faster! I'll {{b10}} the things.
Dad: Great. Go ahead. And yes — you can get the magazine. You were a big help today.

blanks.explanation: A 'shopping list' is a written record of all the items you need to buy.
Lily spots the milk 'next to the butter' — both are dairy products kept together.
An 'aisle' is a long corridor between shelves in a supermarket.
Dad says wholemeal is 'healthier' — it contains more fibre and nutrients.
The 'fridge section' is the refrigerated area of the supermarket where eggs, milk, and cheese are kept cold.
'Firm' means hard and solid — a sign that the fruit is fresh and not overripe.
Dad chooses 'penne' — a short tube-shaped pasta.
Lily asks if she can buy 'a magazine' — a small reward for helping with the shopping.
A 'till' is a checkout counter operated by a cashier — the alternative to self-checkout.
'Scan' means to pass items over the barcode reader at the self-checkout machine.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Dad lets Lily help choose items and make decisions at the supermarket. Do you think it is good for children to join in with everyday tasks like grocery shopping? What practical life skills can they learn from it?
